post.description:
Create a new URL alias for the current customer.

If ``is_routing`` is True (the default) and another alias is already
routing the same (name, request_routing_key), this one is created as
non-routing instead. Users can later switch routing via the
``/aliases/{id}/switch`` endpoint.

get.description: List customer's aliases, optionally filtered by name.
Get a specific alias by ID.

patch.description: Update an alias's target path or routing key override.

delete.description: Deactivate an alias (sets deactivated_at for billing proration).

post.description:
Switch routing on or off for this alias.

When ``on=True``, any sibling alias currently routing the same
(name, routing_key) combo is atomically demoted. The response
includes ``demoted_alias_id`` so the caller knows what changed.

When ``on=False``, the alias stops routing. No sibling is
auto-promoted — the combo will have no routing alias until one
is explicitly switched on.

Billing is unaffected — all non-deactivated aliases accrue duration
regardless of routing state.

Create a draft recurrent request.

Returns the created record with its ID, which can be used as the
X-Playground-Request header for test requests.

get.description: List recurrent requests for the current customer.
Get a recurrent request by ID.

patch.description:
Update a recurrent request (template, schedule, name, or status).

Setting a schedule on a draft transitions it to active and creates
a RedBeat entry for periodic execution.

delete.description: Delete a recurrent request and remove its RedBeat entry.

post.description:
Execute a recurrent request once via the gateway proxy.

Resolves body templates, sends the request through execute_proxy_request
(same code path as the gateway and recurrence worker), and returns the
upstream response directly.

get.description:
List the current customer's secrets.

Returns secret metadata only — values are never returned.

Get metadata for one of the current customer's secrets by name.

Returns metadata only — the value is never returned.

put.description:
Set a customer secret to ``value`` (idempotent create-or-replace).

Returns ``201 Created`` on insert and ``200 OK`` on update.
The value is encrypted at rest. **The value cannot be retrieved
after this call** — store it securely if you need a copy.

The customer's routing cache is invalidated so the gateway picks
up the new value immediately (and any pending enrollments that
were blocked waiting for this secret can now resolve).

delete.description:
Delete a customer secret by name.

**Warning:** this action cannot be undone. Any active enrollments
that reference this secret will stop working immediately. The
customer's routing cache is invalidated.

RecurrentRequestCreate.title: RecurrentRequestCreate
RecurrentRequestCreate.description:
Schema for creating a RecurrentRequest (draft).

``request_headers`` is the raw dict of headers sent on each execution
and is the **single source of truth** for per-request opt-ins such as
``X-Unitysvc-Log-Request``. Higher-level clients (frontend, CLI) are
responsible for computing the final dict — this schema does not
accept any convenience flags that would mutate it server-side.

RecurrentRequestStatusEnum.title: RecurrentRequestStatusEnum
RecurrentRequestStatusEnum.description:
Status of a recurrent request.

Lifecycle: draft → active → paused → cancelled

RecurrentRequestUpdate.title: RecurrentRequestUpdate
RecurrentRequestUpdate.description:
Schema for updating a RecurrentRequest.

``request_headers`` replaces the stored dict wholesale when provided
(partial/PATCH semantics apply at the top level, not inside the dict).
See :class:`RecurrentRequestCreate` for the rationale.

RecurrentRequestsPublic.title: RecurrentRequestsPublic
RecurrentRequestsPublic.description: List of RecurrentRequests for API responses.

SecretOwnerTypeEnum.title: SecretOwnerTypeEnum
SecretOwnerTypeEnum.description: Owner type for secrets - determines which entity owns the secret.

name.title: Name
name.description: Secret name (e.g., OPENAI_API_KEY). Must be uppercase letters, digits, and underscores; must start with a letter or underscore.

SecretPublic.title: SecretPublic
SecretPublic.description: Public schema for secret info (excludes value - write-only).

value.title: Value
value.description: Secret value (will be encrypted). May be empty.

SecretUpdate.title: SecretUpdate
SecretUpdate.description:
Request body for ``PUT /secrets/{name}``.

Carries only the value — the name comes from the URL path. The same
schema is used for both create and update because ``PUT`` is
idempotent (see issue #798).

Empty string is allowed: a customer may deliberately store ``""``
to override a non-empty default in a ``${ secrets.X ?? default }``
reference. ``??`` coalesces on null only, so the explicit empty
value is preserved.

name.title: Name
name.description: URL-safe alias name (lowercase, alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores)

is_routing.title: Is Routing
is_routing.description: Whether to make this alias the active routing one for its (name, routing_key) combo. Fails with 409 if another alias is already routing.

ServiceAliasUpdate.title: ServiceAliasUpdate
ServiceAliasUpdate.description:
Schema for updating a ServiceAlias.

The alias name cannot be changed (it's the URL path segment).
All other fields — including request_routing_key — are updatable.
A unique constraint check runs at save time; conflicts return 409.

Routing state is managed via the dedicated ``POST /switch`` endpoint,
not through this schema.

demoted_alias_id.title: Demoted Alias Id
demoted_alias_id.description: ID of the sibling alias that was demoted when switching on. Null when switching off or when no sibling was routing.
